title: Momentum
short: Mass in motion, p = m·v, measured in kg·m/s. Conserved when no external force acts.
full:
Momentum is the product of an object's mass and velocity, p = m·v. It is a vector pointing along the velocity, and it measures how hard it is to stop a moving object — a heavy truck and a fast bullet can carry comparable momentum.

Newton's second law in its general form says force is the rate of change of momentum, F = Δp / Δt. It follows that when the net external force is zero the total momentum of a system is conserved, which is the key tool for analysing collisions and explosions.

The SI unit is the kilogram-metre per second (kg·m/s).
